title: The Imperfect Subjunctive
description: Form the imperfect (past) subjunctive from the ellos-preterite, master the -ra and -se forms, and use it for the sequence of tenses, second-conditional si-clauses, como si and ojalá.

cards.back: build the imperfect subjunctive from the ellos-preterite: drop -ron, add -ra or -se
cards.notes: Take the **ellos** preterite (*hablaron, comieron, vivieron*), drop **-ron**, and add the endings. The **-ra** and **-se** sets are interchangeable.

cards.back: the -ra endings (note the accent on nosotros: habláramos)
cards.notes: The nosotros form takes a written accent: *habláramos, comiéramos, tuviéramos*. The -se set mirrors it: *hablase, hablases…*

cards.front: tuvieron → tuviera; fueron → fuera; hicieron → hiciera
cards.back: irregular preterites carry their stem into the imperfect subjunctive
cards.notes: Because we start from the ellos-preterite, every irregular preterite comes along: *tener → tuviera*, *ser/ir → fuera*, *hacer → hiciera*, *decir → dijera*, *poder → pudiera*, *poner → pusiera*.
Sequence of tenses: a **past** main verb (*quería*) needs the **past** subjunctive (*vinieras*), not the present.

cards.back: If I had time, I would travel.
cards.notes: Second conditional (unreal present): **Si + imperfecto de subjuntivo**, **condicional simple**.

cards.back: as if / as though (+ imperfect subjunctive)
cards.notes: *Habla como si fuera un experto.* — He talks as if he were an expert. Always followed by the past subjunctive.

cards.back: I wish I had more time. / If only I had more time.
cards.notes: **Ojalá + imperfecto de subjuntivo** = a wish about the present that is unlikely or untrue.

cards.back: imperfect subjunctive of ser AND ir (same form)
cards.notes: *ser* and *ir* share the form *fuera/fuese* — context decides the meaning.

steps.title: Forming the imperfect subjunctive
steps.body:
# The imperfect (past) subjunctive

The rule is mechanical and never fails:

1. Take the **ellos** form of the **preterite**: *hablar → hablaron*, *comer → comieron*, *tener → tuvieron*.
2. Drop **-ron**.
3. Add the endings (two interchangeable sets):

| | -ra set | -se set |
|---|---|---|
| yo | hablara | hablase |
| tú | hablaras | hablases |
| él/ella | hablara | hablase |
| nosotros | **habláramos** | **hablásemos** |
| vosotros | hablarais | hablaseis |
| ellos | hablaran | hablasen |

Note the **accent** on *nosotros*. Because we start from the preterite, **every** irregular preterite carries over: *tuvieron → tuviera*, *fueron → fuera*, *hicieron → hiciera*, *dijeron → dijera*.

steps.title: When to use it
steps.body:
# Four key uses

## 1. Sequence of tenses

A **past** main clause requires the **past** subjunctive:

- *Quiero que vengas.* (present → present)
- *Quería que **vinieras**.* — I wanted you to come. (past → past)

## 2. Second conditional (unreal present)

> **Si + imperfecto de subjuntivo, condicional simple**

- *Si **tuviera** tiempo, **viajaría**.* — If I had time, I would travel.

Never use the present subjunctive after this *si* — and never *si tuviera… viajara*.

## 3. como si (as if)

- *Habla **como si fuera** un experto.* — He talks as if he were an expert.

## 4. ojalá (I wish)

- ***Ojalá tuviera** más tiempo.* — I wish I had more time.

exercise.prompt: Match each infinitive with its yo imperfect-subjunctive form.
Fill in the imperfect subjunctive of venir (tú): "I wanted you to come."
Fill in the imperfect subjunctive of tener (yo): "If I had time, I would travel."
Translate: *If I were rich, I would travel the world.* (ser → fuera; viajar → conditional)
accept: Si fuera rico, viajaría por el mundo.
si fuera rico, viajaría por el mundo
Si fuera rico, viajaría por el mundo.
Si fuese rico, viajaría por el mundo.
distractors: Si era rico, viajaría por el mundo.
Si fuera rico, viajara por el mundo.
Si sería rico, viajaría por el mundo.

exercise.prompt: Arrange: *He talks as if he were an expert.*
exercise.hint: como si always takes the imperfect subjunctive (fuera).

exercise.prompt: Translate: *I wish I had more time.* (Ojalá + imperfect subjunctive of tener)
